text: RAC Security Services (WA) Pty Ltd Trading as RAC Security Services ABN 44 096 235 200 Police Licence No 45251 T 1300 360 504 F (08) 9436 5131 P GPO Box C140, Perth, WA 6839 rac.com.au Monitoring Direct Debit Service Agreement This agreement outlines our service commitment to you, in respect of the Direct Debit Request (“DDR”) made to RACWA Holdings Pty Ltd (user ID 250633) signed by you. It sets out your rights, our commitment to you and your responsibilities to us together with where you should go for assistance. 1. In terms of the DDR, we will periodically debit your nominated account for the agreed amount from time to time for fees for Monitoring Services (“Fees”) payable to RAC Security Services pursuant to the Agreement for Monitoring Services between you and RAC Security Services (WA) Pty Ltd (“AMS”). 2. The payments under this Direct Debit Services Agreement will occur on each anniversary date since services commenced (the date Alarm Monitoring activated) depending on the chosen payment cycle (Monthly, Quarterly, Half Yearly or Annually). 3. If the due date for payment falls on a non-business day or on a public holiday the drawing will be processed on the next business day. 4. We will give you four (4) weeks notice in writing when changes to the Fees payable under the AMS are made. This notice will state the new amount, frequency, next drawing date or any other changes to the Fees. 5. If you want to make any changes to the arrangements under this Direct Debit Service Agreement, please contact us in writing. This must be done at least seven (7) days prior to the next drawing. Changes to the arrangements do not affect your obligations under the AMS to pay the Fees to RAC Security Services. 6. If you believe that a drawing has been initiated incorrectly, we encourage you to take the matter up directly with us. Should you not receive a satisfactory response to your dispute, contact your financial institution who will respond to you with an answer to your claim within the time frame specified in its customer charter of the Banking Code of Practice. 7. It is your responsibility to ensure that: – the account you have nominated in the DDR can accept direct debits (your financial institution can confirm this); – you have sufficient cleared funds in the nominated account on the drawing date; – you advise us in writing if the nominated account is transferred or closed; and – you advise us in writing if you wish to cancel or alter your DDR. 8. If your drawing is returned or dishonoured by your financial institution, we will contact you within even (7) days to arrange an alternative method of payment. You may be charged a fee for each unpaid item. 9. All personal customer information held by us will be kept confidential, except for information provided to our financial institution to initiate the drawing to your nominated account. 10. Direct all inquiries to RAC Security Services. All communication addressed to us should include your client reference number. 11. We reserve the right to revise the terms and conditions of this Direct Debit Service Agreement provided that any revision that is detrimental to you will be notified to you, and you will have the right to terminate the agreement without penalty. 12. Reference to “we” “us” and “our” in this agreement means either or both of RAC Security Services and RACWA Holdings Pty Ltd. 13. The RAC Group Privacy Policy is detailed in its website by visiting www.rac.com.au or by phoning 1300 360 504 and selecting 0 on the options. Should a copy of the statement be required this may also be requested any time during RAC office hours. Terms and Conditions 1. Definitions ‘Client/Company’ – means the person or persons who have requested RAC to provide the Services. ‘Decommissioning‘ – means the termination of active communication between the alarm system and the monitoring station. ‘Fees’ – mean the fees payable by the Client/Company for the Services being RAC’s standard fees for providing the Services as varied from time to time and notified to the Client/Company, inclusive of GST. ‘RAC Group’ – means RAC and its related and subsidiary companies, including RAC Insurance Pty Ltd. ‘Security Patrol Officer’ – means the person nominated by the RAC and despatched in accordance with clause 13. ‘Services’ – mean the monitoring services to be provided to the Client/Company as set out in clause 13 of this Agreement. ‘Term’ – means the agreed monitoring period. 2. RAC Security Services (WA) Pty Ltd (‘RAC’) will provide the Services to the Client/Company in accordance with these terms and conditions. 3. The Client/Company agrees to pay to RAC the Fees periodically in advance. If payment is in arrears at any time RAC shall not be bound to perform part or all of the Services. Payment may be made by one of the following payment methods: direct debit or on receipt of invoice. 4. The Fees paid by the Client/Company to RAC do not include the cost of a Security Patrol Officer response to alarm conditions, Decommissioning, taxes (other than GST),charges and fees imposed by the Government, Statutory Authorities, or the costs charged by communication carriers (‘Carriers’). 5. Upon completion of the initial Term, the Client/Company may give thirty (30) day’s written notice of its intention to terminate this agreement. For the avoidance of doubt, it is the responsibility of the Client/Company to provide RAC with written notice of termination. 6. RAC may alter Fees by giving four (4) weeks written notice to the Client/Company stating the new amount , any other changes to the Fees and the date from which the change will apply. 7. RAC reserves the right to revise these terms and conditions provided that any revision that is detrimental to the Client/Company will be notified to the Client/Company, and, in the case of detriment, the Client/Company will have the right to terminate the agreement without penalty. 8. RAC will not be liable for any costs associated with the Decommissioning of the alarm system, nor any costs relating to the continued telecommunication once the service has been terminated. It is the obligation of the Client/Company to advise any future owners or tenants of the property of the potential for continued telecommunications and associated costs should the alarm system not be Decommissioned. 9. Where the Client/Company authorises RAC to apply to a Carrier for any communication services necessary to provide the Services, the Client/Company must pay all charges levied by the Carrier to ensure continuity of the Services. 10. Pursuant to s.64 of the Australian Consumer Law (Cth) (‘ACL’), this clause applies in respect of any of the Services which are not of a kind ordinarily acquired for personal, domestic or household use or consumption, provided that this clause will not apply if the Client/Company establishes that reliance on it would not be fair or reasonable. Liability for breach of a condition or warranty implied into this agreement by the ACL is limited in the case of Services to supplying the Services, or the payment of the cost of having the Services supplied again. 11. Subject to any implied terms, conditions and warranties imposed by the ACL, RAC shall not be liable to the Client/Company for any injury, death, loss or damage including loss of profits and consequential loss suffered by the Client/Company howsoever caused, including but not limited to injury, death, loss or damage caused by the negligence of RAC, its employees, servants or agents, relating in any way to the provision of the Services hereunder but not limited to failing to follow up any signal or alarm or by non-receipt of any alarm. 12. RAC will not be liable for any loss or damage suffered by the Client/Company and caused by the RAC’s delay or failure to provide the Services due to industrial disputes, shortage of labour and materials, riots, natural disasters, or any other cause beyond RAC’s control. 13. RAC will offer the following Service in the event an alarm is activated: 13.1 Notify the contact(s) nominated by the Client/Company. 13.2 In the event the nominated contact(s) cannot be contacted, a Security Patrol Officer will be despatched in accordance with the Client/ Company’s recorded instructions established at the time of commissioning. The cost for this service is to be borne by the Client/Company. 14. The Client/Company must ensure that all their equipment which is necessary for RAC to provide the Services is maintained in accordance with the operating instructions supplied with the equipment and the routine maintenance recommended in the Australian Standards AS2201 (equipment maintained at a minimum rate of once (1) per year with no more than thirteen (13) months between visits). RAC’s obligations to provide the Services are suspended during any period in which the Client/Company is in breach of this clause. 15. The Client /Company shall advise RAC in writing if during the Term the nominate contact(s) number(s) change. 16. If a party is in breach of any of these terms and conditions and does not rectify that breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving notice in writing to do so from the other party, then the other party is entitled to terminate this agreement by written notice effective immediately. 17. On termination of this agreement under clause 16,the Client/Company must pay RAC all Fees which would otherwise fall due for payment prior to or on the date of termination. 18. The Services come with certain consumer guarantees which cannot be excluded under the ACL. The guarantees are in addition to other rights and remedies which may be legally available. 19. RAC may assign its rights or subcontract whole or part of the Services under this agreement. 20. These terms and conditions prevail over any inconsistent terms in any other document. 21. The RAC Group Privacy Policy is detailed in its website by visiting www.rac.com.au or by phoning 1300 360 504 and selecting 0 on the options. Should a copy of the statement be required this may also be requested any time during RAC office hours.
